dialogue: A weak voice cuts through the noise. 'Is... anyone out there? Please, I'm trapped.'

choices.lockedText: You can't make out enough detail to know if this is safe.

choices.text: Boost the signal to trace the source
choices.lockedText: You don't have the technical knowledge to boost the signal.

dialogue: The voice gains clarity. 'Oh thank god. I'm in the old warehouse on the east road.'
'Three days. I have supplies. But I can hear them outside the door.'

choices.text: Tell them to stay quiet and sit tight

choices.lockedText: You don't have the wilderness knowledge to come up with anything useful.

choices.text: You're on your own. I can't risk it.

dialogue: The signal resolves to coordinates — a warehouse two kilometres east.
The broadcast loops on repeat. They may not even know you heard them.

choices.text: Mark the location and head there
Broadcast back to confirm someone is alive
choices.lockedText: You hesitate — you've never been good at knowing what to say.

dialogue: 'Okay. Okay, I can do that.' A long silence follows.
'Please hurry. I don't know how long the battery lasts.'
You walk them through it — noise from the far side of the building, timed movement.
'That... might actually work. I'll try it. Thank you.' The channel goes quiet.
For a moment, you hear them calling out again. Then nothing.
You turn down the volume. It wasn't your problem to begin with.
